prompt: Which site is generally preferred for an adult intramuscular injection of a routine vaccine dose?

options.text: Deltoid muscle (upper arm) when volume is appropriate
Gluteus maximus without landmarking
Subcutaneous tissue of the abdomen only
Intravenous antecubital vein

explanation: For many adult IM vaccines, the deltoid is preferred when the volume fits; proper landmarking avoids nerves/vessels. Route must match the order.

distractorExplanations.b: Dorsogluteal without landmarks risks sciatic nerve injury and is often avoided. Learners who choose “Gluteus maximus without landmarking” are usually making that exact mistake instead of applying the rule in the stem.
distractorExplanations.c: Abdomen is a common SQ site, not the default IM vaccine site. Learners who choose “Subcutaneous tissue of the abdomen only” are usually making that exact mistake instead of applying the rule in the stem.
distractorExplanations.d: IV is a different route requiring specific authorization/training. Learners who choose “Intravenous antecubital vein” are usually making that exact mistake instead of applying the rule in the stem.

prompt: When measuring an adult oral temperature with an electronic probe, the CMA should:

explanation: Oral electronic thermometers read from the sublingual pocket; technique differs for tympanic/temporal devices.

distractorExplanations.c: Ear probes are tympanic, not oral. Learners who choose “Insert the probe into the ear canal for oral readings” are usually making that exact mistake instead of applying the rule in the stem.
distractorExplanations.d: Ice water would falsify the reading. Learners who choose “Leave the probe in ice water first” are usually making that exact mistake instead of applying the rule in the stem.

prompt: Which vital sign change most urgently suggests possible orthostatic hypotension when a patient stands?

prompt: Which statement about sterile fields is correct?
